message:
refactor(stdio): route printf conversion classification through PrintfDispatchKind enum

Introduces a dispatch-kind taxonomy over printf conversion bytes so
the abi layer stops open-coding `match spec.conversion { b'%' | b'm' => ..., b'f' | b'F' | b'e' | ... }`
at multiple call sites.

- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs exposes a new public
  `PrintfDispatchKind` enum with variants LiteralPercent, ErrnoMessage,
  StoreCount, SignedInt, UnsignedInt, Float, Character, String, Pointer.
  The paired `pub fn printf_dispatch_kind(conversion: u8) ->
  Option<PrintfDispatchKind>` first special-cases `b'm'` (errno
  message — `%m` has no printf_route entry) then delegates to
  `printf_route(conversion)` and maps the handler to its kind:
    * LiteralPercent / StoreCount pass through unchanged.
    * SignedDecimal → SignedInt.
    * UnsignedOctal / UnsignedDecimal / UnsignedHex / UnsignedBinary
      → UnsignedInt.
    * Float / FloatScientific / FloatShortest / FloatHex → Float.
    * Character → Character.
    * String → String.
    * Pointer → Pointer.

- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs switches three open-coded
  conversion matches (spec_value_kind, count_printf_args, the
  extract_va_args! macro arm) to call `printf_dispatch_kind` and
  match on the returned enum:
    * LiteralPercent / ErrnoMessage → emit no va_arg consumption.
    * Float → emit PrintfArgKind::Fp.
    * every other kind → emit PrintfArgKind::Gp.
  Imports grow to pull in `printf_dispatch_kind` and `PrintfDispatchKind`
  from the stdio crate.

Central benefit: adding a new printf conversion byte now requires
touching PrintfHandler + printf_dispatch_kind once; abi's va_arg
classification follows automatically instead of needing parallel
match arms. No change in emitted printf output for any existing
conversion specifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
